Description: An open source web browser developed by the Mozilla Foundation. Contributors help fix bugs, improve browser performance, build new features, and refine the user interface across desktop and mobile platforms.
Roles Required: Frontend Engineer, Backend Engineer, UI Designer, UX Researcher, QA Tester, Technical Writer

Description: An open source container orchestration platform originally developed by Google that automates deployment, scaling, and management of containerized applications. Widely used in cloud-native development and supported by thousands of contributors worldwide.
Roles Required: Backend Engineer, DevOps Engineer, Cloud Architect, Documentation Writer, Researcher, QA Tester

Description: A series of open source short films (Sintel, Cosmos Laundromat, Sprite Fright, Charge, Wing It!) produced by Blender Studio to push the Blender 3D software forward. All assets, rigs, models, and source files are released under Creative Commons so the global community can learn from and reuse them.
Roles Required: Director, 3D Modeler, Animator, Videographer, Editor, VFX Artist, Concept Artist, Sound Designer, Composer

Description: A massive open archive of public domain and Creative Commons films, documentaries, and historical footage. Volunteers help digitize, tag, restore, and preserve films so they remain freely accessible to the public for educational and creative use.
Roles Required: Videographer, Editor, Archivist, Researcher, Metadata Specialist, Graphic Designer, Restoration Specialist

Description: An open source investigative journalism community where volunteers verify social media footage, geolocate images, and authenticate visual evidence for real-world investigations. Contributors support reporting on conflicts, human rights abuses, and disinformation.
Roles Required: Researcher, OSINT Investigator, Fact-Checker, Writer, Graphic Designer, Videographer, Data Analyst

Description: An international community of writers, translators, and digital activists who report on stories underrepresented by mainstream media. Volunteers translate articles into dozens of languages and produce original reporting from their home countries.
Roles Required: Writer, Editor, Translator, Researcher, Graphic Designer, Social Media Manager, Videographer

Description: A joint initiative of the California Academy of Sciences and National Geographic where users worldwide log observations of plants and animals. The platform has contributed over 200 million biodiversity records to the Global Biodiversity Information Facility (GBIF), supporting real scientific research.
Roles Required: Researcher, Field Observer, Species Identifier, UI Designer, Frontend Engineer, Data Scientist, Community Moderator

Description: A crowdsourced protein-folding puzzle game developed by the University of Washington. Players have helped design novel proteins and solve real protein structures, with results published in peer-reviewed journals like Nature. Contributors range from gamers to trained biochemists.
Roles Required: Researcher, Biochemist, Game Designer, UI Designer, Frontend Engineer, Community Manager, Data Analyst
